title: deep_merge unbounded recursion and array concatenation explosion
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension**: Performance & Complexity / Security (DoS)

**Location**: src/hooks/merge.rs:75-90 (deep_merge)

**Violation**: deep_merge is a naive recursive function with:
- No recursion-depth limit — a maliciously deeply nested JSON settings file (e.g. 100k levels) overflows the stack.
- Arrays concatenate without upper bound (merge.rs:83-85) — a hostile managed-settings file can append infinitely to, e.g., hooks.PreToolUse causing OOM.
- load_claude_settings (hooks/claude_compat.rs:108-185) applies FOUR merge_settings_file calls in sequence without validating total accumulated size.
- When the project-level settings.local.json is gitignored/not-checked-in, an attacker who gains write on the developer machine has unbounded influence.

**Mandated Refactor**:
1. Convert deep_merge to iterative using an explicit work stack; enforce MAX_DEPTH=32.
2. Enforce MAX_SETTINGS_BYTES (e.g. 1 MiB) before calling serde_json::from_str in merge_settings_file.
3. Enforce MAX_ARRAY_LEN per array after merge (e.g. 1024).
4. Return Result<(), MergeError> from deep_merge instead of mutating in place; surface errors in merge_settings_file.
